title: A stray SpaceX rocket stage could slam into the moon this August, amateur astronomer says
summary: A stray SpaceX rocket stage from a 2021 Falcon 9 launch is predicted to collide with the moon in early August. The impact is expected to occur due to the rocket's uncontrolled trajectory.

title: Help scientists find spacetime warps in these Euclid Space Telescope images
summary: A new citizen science project invites the public to analyze images from the Euclid Space Telescope to identify galaxies that bend spacetime. This effort aims to help scientists study gravitational lensing and dark matter.

title: Is Tatooine the norm? Planets may prefer living with two suns instead of one
summary: New simulations suggest that binary star systems might be more conducive to planet formation than single-star systems, potentially producing more gas giants.

title: NASA chief Jared Isaacman says he's fighting for Pluto: 'I am very much in the camp of 'make Pluto a planet again'
summary: NASA chief Jared Isaacman advocates for Pluto's reclassification as a planet, stating he supports 'making Pluto a planet again.' NASA is considering revisiting the discussion on Pluto's planethood status.

title: These astronauts are trying to uphold the US Constitution: 'We need to make sure that people are using facts and evidence'
summary: Astronauts emphasize the importance of using facts and evidence while exploring space, highlighting the role of wonder in expanding human perspective. They discuss the need to uphold the US Constitution in their mission.

title: Artemis 3 SLS rocket's core stage arrives in Florida for 2027 launch (photo)
summary: The core stage of NASA's Artemis 3 SLS rocket has arrived at Kennedy Space Center in Florida after a 900-mile barge journey, marking a key milestone ahead of its planned 2027 launch.

title: Clean up Rebel scum in the upcoming Star Wars DLC for PowerWash Simulator 2
summary: PowerWash Simulator 2 will release a Star Wars-themed DLC. Players can clean up Rebel scum with soap and water.

title: Canadian astronaut Josh Kutryk finally flying to ISS after Boeing Starliner mishap: 'I'm committed to making the most of this unique opportunity'
summary: Canadian astronaut Josh Kutryk has been assigned to the Crew-13 mission to the ISS after the Boeing Starliner mishap delayed his original flight. This comes shortly after Canada's participation in the Artemis 2 moon mission.

title: NASA Connects Little Red Dots with Chandra, Webb
summary: NASA's Chandra X-ray Observatory discovered a new object that may help explain mysterious 'X-ray dots' found in the early universe. This discovery could clarify the nature of hundreds or thousands of similar objects detected in recent years.

title: There’s No Place Like NASA’s New X-59 Hangar Home
summary: NASA's newly renovated X-59 hangar at Armstrong Flight Research Center in Edwards, California, houses the quiet supersonic aircraft. The 1968-built hangar was fully renovated to look like new.

title: Nighttime Imaging Grows Landsat’s Science Value
summary: Landsat satellites have been imaging Earth's land and near-shore surfaces for over 50 years during midmorning orbits. Now, Landsat 8 and Landsat 9 are also utilizing their ascending paths to enhance nighttime imaging capabilities.
company: Earth Resources Observation and Science (EROS) Center

title: Curiosity Captures a 360-Degree View at ‘Nevado Sajama’
summary: NASA's Curiosity Mars rover captured a high-resolution 360-degree panorama of 'Nevado Sajama' region on Mars, featuring boxwork formations. This 1.5 billion-pixel image is one of the largest panoramas the rover has ever taken.

title: NASA Fires Up Powerful Lithium-Fed Thruster for Trips to Mars
summary: NASA successfully tested a powerful lithium-fed thruster at the Jet Propulsion Laboratory, which could enable crewed missions to Mars and robotic exploration across the solar system. The test marked the highest power levels achieved in the U.S. in years.

title: NASA Laser Terminal Enhances Views During Artemis II Mission
summary: NASA's laser terminal improved views during the Artemis II mission, allowing millions to witness the historic 10-day journey around the Moon with astronauts Reid Wiseman, Victor Glover, Christina Koch, and CSA's Jeremy Hansen.

title: Six Years of Curiosity’s Wheels on the Move
summary: NASA's Curiosity Mars rover captured a timelapse of its six years of driving using its right navigation camera. The images were taken between January 2, 2020, and March 8, 2026.

title: Science in Space
summary: NASA astronaut Chris Williams and ESA astronaut Sophie Adenot are collaborating on the DNA Nano Therapeutics-3 experiment in the Kibo laboratory module, investigating DNA-inspired assembly techniques for potential cancer treatments.

title: Artemis III core stage assembly arrives at the Kennedy Space Center
summary: The core stage assembly for NASA's Artemis III mission has arrived at the Kennedy Space Center. This follows the recent Artemis II launch, which marked a historic crewed flyby of the Moon.

title: Launch Preview: Falcon Heavy returns, Atlas V and Ariane 6 to launch Amazon Leo satellites
summary: Falcon Heavy, Atlas V, and Ariane 6 are set to launch Amazon's Leo satellites this week from multiple locations. SpaceX's Falcon Heavy is returning for the mission alongside ULA's Atlas V and Arianespace's Ariane 6.

title: SpaceX set to launch Falcon Heavy on Wednesday after 18 month hiatus
summary: SpaceX is preparing to launch its Falcon Heavy rocket after an 18-month hiatus. This marks a significant return for the heavy-lift vehicle following its last mission.

title: Curiosity Rover uncovers fresh organic clues on Ancient Mars
summary: NASA's Curiosity Rover has discovered new organic molecules on Mars, providing fresh clues about ancient life on the Red Planet. This finding marks a significant advancement in the search for extraterrestrial life.

title: Progress MS-34 to launch from Baikonur for ISS cargo delivery
summary: Progress MS-34 is scheduled to launch from Baikonur Cosmodrome to deliver cargo to the International Space Station (ISS). This follows the CRS NG-24 mission, which recently launched cargo to the ISS.

title: Starship Progress at the Cape with Hoping to Launch this year
summary: SpaceX is making progress on the Starship program at Cape Canaveral, with hopes to launch the Block 3 variant later this year. The first flight of Block 3 is approaching as the company continues its development efforts.

title: NASA and Axiom react to OIG Report on delays in Next-Generation Spacesuit Program
summary: NASA and Axiom Space have responded to a critical report from NASA's Office of Inspector General highlighting delays in the Next-Generation Spacesuit Program. The report raises concerns about the program's timeline and potential impacts on future missions.

title: Launch Preview: GPS, Progress, and Starlink missions to launch during busy week
summary: A busy week in space launches is expected with seven missions planned, involving four countries and five different launch vehicles. The missions include GPS, Progress, and Starlink deployments.

title: AST SpaceMobile’s BlueBird 7 declared lost following New Glenn launch
summary: AST SpaceMobile's BlueBird 7 satellite was declared lost after the New Glenn launch. This follows Blue Origin's achievement of landing a previously flown first-stage booster.

title: New Glenn launches on third mission, successfully reuses booster for the first time
summary: Blue Origin's New Glenn rocket successfully completed its third mission and reused a booster for the first time. The launch occurred just five months after its second mission.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) released a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over its 50-year history. This commemorative publication celebrates the agency's milestones and contributions to space exploration.

title: A new grip on space: electrostatic capture technology
summary: A new electrostatic capture technology has been developed for capturing objects in space. This innovation could revolutionize satellite servicing and debris removal.

title: Plato aces space-like tests
summary: The European Space Agency's Plato mission has passed critical space-like tests, confirming it is on track for a launch in early 2027 to search for Earth-like planets.

title: SME Forum 2026 – Join ESA's annual consultation process
summary: ESA invites SMEs and midcaps to participate in its annual consultation process, including an SME Forum on 8–9 June 2026 at ESA Headquarters in Paris, preceded by online sessions in April and May.

title: The perfect place for Celeste’s first navigation signal
summary: Celeste's first navigation signal was successfully received, marking a significant milestone for the project. The location for this achievement was highlighted as ideal.

title: First Proba-3 science: surprisingly speedy solar wind
summary: ESA's Proba-3 mission has conducted 57 artificial solar eclipses since July 2025, collecting over 250 hours of high-resolution solar corona data. The mission reveals that solar wind structures in the inner corona travel 3-4 times faster than previously believed.

title: Register for ESA’s Industry Space Days 2026
summary: Registration is now open for ESA's Industry Space Days 2026, an event scheduled for 16–17 September at the agency's technical centre in Noordwijk, The Netherlands.

title: Pointing in the (b)right direction
summary: The article discusses a new development in spacecraft pointing technology. It highlights advancements in precision pointing systems for space missions.

title: Graphene and lasers for space propulsion
summary: An international research team demonstrated light-based propulsion using graphene aerogels during ESA's parabolic flight campaign in May 2025. The study, published in Advanced Science, showed that lasers can propel graphene aerogels efficiently in microgravity, offering potential for future satellite and solar sail applications.

title: DPhi’s Second Demo Brings Compute Space to Orbit
summary: DPhi Space successfully demonstrated in-orbit compute by running Liquid AI's large language model on an EO image without transmitting it to Earth. The mission, Clustergate-2, launched on Momentus' Vigoride 7 satellite in March.

title: Golden Dome Will Use COTS Parts, Space Execs Say
summary: Golden Dome satellite bus will utilize Commercial Off-The-Shelf (COTS) parts, according to executives. Apex Space CEO Ian Cinnamon emphasized that it aims to offer no unique differentiators compared to existing commercial satellite buses.

title: Dems Slam Trump Over NSF Board Cuts
summary: Democrats criticize Trump's administration for proposed cuts to the National Science Foundation (NSF) board, calling it harmful to science and innovation. The criticism comes from Rep. Zoe Lofgren (D-CA) in response to the latest policy move.

title: Isaacman Continues to Defend Budget Cuts to Congress
summary: Isaacman defended budget cuts to Congress while emphasizing the agency's priority to accelerate the US return to the Moon. The hearing provided further context on these budget adjustments and their implications.

title: True Anomaly Closes $650M Series D
summary: True Anomaly, a four-year-old space company, has raised $650 million in a Series D funding round. The funding follows the company's recent Golden Dome award for its space-based interceptor technology.

title: Meta Secures Overview Energy Space Solar Power Capacity
summary: Meta has secured an agreement to obtain up to 1 GW of power capacity through Overview Energy's planned solar power-beaming satellite system.

title: Pentagon Taps 12 Companies for Golden Dome SBI Tech
summary: The Pentagon selected 12 companies for the Golden Dome SBI Tech program, awarding contracts worth up to $3.2 billion in total. This initiative aims to bolster space-based defense technologies.

title: Astra Targets Golden Dome With Small Rockets, Says CEO Chris Kemp
summary: Astra's CEO Chris Kemp announced plans to use small rockets for target practice, aiming to reduce costs for commercial and government customers. The initiative is part of Astra's strategy to scale operations and improve efficiency.

title: Trump Taps Space Execs For Military Space Roles
summary: Donald Trump has appointed space industry executives to key military space roles. The nominees require Senate confirmation before assuming their positions.

title: Astrobotic Hotfires Engine That Could Power Moon Missions
summary: Astrobotic successfully test-fired a Chakram engine prototype for five minutes at NASA's Marshall Space Flight Center. The engine could potentially power future moon missions.

title: True Anomaly Raises $650 Million in New Round to Continue Scaling
summary: True Anomaly secured $650 million in a new funding round to expand its workforce and scale operations to meet growing customer demand.

title: ST Engineering Signs Singapore Public Safety Deal with HTX
summary: ST Engineering and HTX will establish a new space technology program to co-develop capabilities for Singapore's public safety operations. The collaboration aims to strengthen national safety through space-based science and technology.

title: MaiaSpace CEO Says “Start Later, Run Faster” as Launcher Works Toward First Mission
summary: MaiaSpace, a French launcher developer, aims to disrupt industry norms by adopting a 'start later, run faster' strategy to bring a new rocket to market for European launch services. The company is actively working toward its first mission.

title: Airbus Defence and Space Improves Revenue and Margin in Q1 2026
summary: Airbus Defence and Space reported improved revenue, adjusted EBIT, and order intake in Q1 2026, nearly doubling its order intake compared to the same period last year.

title: T-Mobile Debuts Managed Broadband Service for Businesses, Combining 5G and Starlink
summary: T-Mobile has launched a new managed broadband service for businesses called Super Broadband, integrating 5G and Starlink satellite internet. This collaboration deepens T-Mobile's partnership with SpaceX.

title: ULA Launches 29 Satellites in 6th Mission for Amazon Leo
summary: United Launch Alliance (ULA) successfully launched 29 satellites for Amazon's Leo constellation using an Atlas V rocket. This marks ULA's sixth mission supporting Amazon's satellite network.

title: Gatehouse Satcom and Rohde & Schwarz Partner on 5GN NTN Testing
summary: Gatehouse Satcom and Rohde & Schwarz have partnered to collaborate on 5G non-terrestrial network (NTN) testing, leveraging Gatehouse's software expertise and Rohde & Schwarz's testing capabilities.

title: HawkEye 360 Files to Go Public, Reports $99M in Revenue in 2025
summary: HawkEye 360, a signals intelligence company, announced plans to go public via an IPO in 2024, projecting $99 million in revenue for 2025.

title: Pakistan Adds to EO Fleet of Satellites
summary: Pakistan has expanded its Earth Observation (EO) satellite fleet with the launch of the EO-3 satellite by SUPARCO. This addition enhances the country's capabilities in monitoring and analyzing environmental and atmospheric data.

title: Space Force Awards $3.2 Billion to 12 Companies for Space-Based Interceptor Work
summary: The U.S. Space Force awarded 12 companies contracts totaling up to $3.2 billion to develop space-based interceptor technologies under the Golden Dome initiative. This marks a significant investment in missile defense capabilities in space.
